Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules MODIFIES tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KEVIN WRIGHT'S Notice Of Joinder In Lyfts Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is moot because Defendant Lyft's Motion for Judgment on the Pledings is denied as moot.

Andrew S. Werner, Esq., Counsel for defendant Kevin J. Wright is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com, Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)

Matter is not contested. Matter not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

3 - PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE FARBER'S Motion For Relief Pursuant To Ccp Section 473 A

Off calendar as moot. The court agreed to entertain the late-filed opposition as part of its ruling on the motion for judgment on the pleadings/motion to compel arbitration.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com, Court reporter, Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Or, In The Alternative, Enforce Lyft, Inc.???S Terms Of Service (Motions)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Samer Habbas, Esq. LAW OFFICES OF SAMER HABBAS & ASSOCIATES, INC., P.C., (949) 727-9300, for Plaintiff Stephanie Farber via Zoom Conference
Spencer H. Kelly, Esq., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, 415-438-5928 Spencer.Kelly@lewisbrisbois.com for Defendant Lyft, Inc.
Andrew S. Werner, Esq., CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, (650) 991-5126, awerner@cwmlaw.com, for Defendant KEVIN J. WRIGHT via Zoom Conference
Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com, Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Or, In The Alternative, Enforce Lyft, Inc.'S Terms Of Service (Motions)

Defendant Lyft's motion to compel arbitration is granted and the court stays this action.

Defendant Lyft's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as moot.

The court entertains plaintiff's opposition since defendant is not prejudiced and replied on the merits.

The parties apparently agree that a binding arbitration agreement governs this dispute. (Plaintiff's Opposition, 19:3-10; Habbas Decl., Ex E [Terms of Service], Sec. 17.) Both sides refer to the arbitration agreement and plaintiff does not raise any defenses to the enforcement of that agreement. Plaintiff merely notes that arbitration should occur "in the State of California in the location closes [sic] to the Plaintiff's city of residence at the time of entering the contract." (Plaintiff's Opposition, 19:7-8.) Therefore, the court orders arbitration and stays this action. Per sec. 17(f) of the terms of service, the arbitration shall occur in the county of plaintiff's billing address and if a AAA facility is unavailable, the nearest available location for a AAA arbitration. (Habbas Decl., Ex E [Terms of Service].)

Order is signed.
